title: How getting the order right changes who you become
caption:
Most men are chasing a wife while God is trying to build a man.

You feel the gap, so you aim at filling it. You lower the bar. You text first. You perform versions of yourself because you think you're running out of time. That energy doesn't stay hidden — it's the loudest thing in the room. She senses it before you open your mouth.

But here's what nobody tells you: the years you spend frustrated in the search are the exact years you could be getting built. The man God is forming right now — in discipline, in the Word, in real brotherhood — is the man she'll one day be drawn to. You can't shortcut formation.

"He who finds a wife finds a good thing." — Proverbs 18:22. Note the verb. He *finds*. You don't manufacture it. You become the kind of man who can build something real, and then you find.

Stop performing. Stop optimizing the pitch. Kill the sin. Show up for your brothers. Be faithful in private when there's no audience. That private faithfulness is what shapes the visible man.

"Delight yourself in the Lord, and he will give you the desires of your heart." — Psalm 37:4. The order is the whole point. Get it right, and the rest takes care of itself.

—

theme: He chases God, not a wife.
